ground_truth:
FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: PA AND LATERAL CHEST X-RAY.

INDICATION: AML, double cord stem cell transplant, immunosuppressant, biliary sepsis, recent hypoxia, assess interval change.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: Multiple chest x-rays from [DATE] to [DATE].

FINDINGS:

Bilateral opacities have not significantly changed since the last exam. Even if part of it could be related to pulmonary edema, more focal areas in right middle and upper lobe should raise concern for superimposed pneumonia. Right jugular line ends in mid SVC. Mediastinal and cardiac contours are normal. There is no pneumothorax or significant pleural effusion.

IMPRESSION:

Bilateral opacities have not changed in extent. As mentioned in earlier exam, part of it could be due to pulmonary edema; however, more focal areas in right lung should raise concern for superimposed infection.

FINAL REPORT
WET READ: WET READ: [AGE/GENDER] [AGE/GENDER] 11:43 PM Within the limitations of frontal radiography, no large change is detected compared to earlier this afternoon. Discussed with Dr. [AGE/GENDER] by phone at 23:41 on [DATE] at time of wet read request. WET READ VERSION #1 ______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: History of loculated pleural effusions and pneumonia. Please evaluate for interval change.

TECHNIQUE: Single AP portable radiograph of the chest.

COMPARISON: CTA from [PRIOR_STUDY] and chest radiographs dated back to [DATE].

FINDINGS:

Right-sided pigtail terminates at the medial right lung base. There has been no significant interval change in the opacification of the mid and lower right lung secondary to large loculated pleural effusions with adjacent atelectasis as described on the recent chest CT. Superimposed infection cannot be excluded. Mild left basilar atelectasis is persistent. There is no evidence of a pneumothorax.

IMPRESSION:

Stable opacification of the mid and lower right lung consistent with large loculated pleural effusions and adjacent atelectasis. Superimposed infectious process cannot be excluded.

WET READ: ___ ___ ___ 3:31 AM
  Decreased size of bilateral pleural effusions. Persistent right upper lobe
  opacity. Pulmonary vascular congestion. Discussed with Dr. ___ by phone at
  3:25 a.m. on ___.
 ______________________________________________________________________________
                                 FINAL REPORT
 CHEST RADIOGRAPH
 
 INDICATION: To look for pulmonary edema.
 
 TECHNIQUE:  Erect portable chest view was read in comparison with prior
 radiograph from ___.
 
 IMPRESSION:
 
 Over last 24 hours, the right upper lobe pneumonia has progressed whereas
 bilateral mild-to-moderate pleural effusions associated with lower lung
 atelectasis have improved. Mild pulmonary vascular congestion is present, but
 no over pulmonary edema. Mildly enlarged heart size, mediastinal and hilar
 contours are unchanged.  Left upper lung is clear.

FINAL REPORT
 HISTORY: History of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, stage I breast cancer,
 presenting with cough and weakness.  Evaluate for pulmonary infiltrates.
 
 COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.
 
 TECHNIQUE:  Frontal and lateral chest radiographs were obtained.
 
 FINDINGS:  The visualized lung fields are clear of any focal opacities,
 pleural effusions or pneumothorax.  Lung markings in the retrocardiac space
 are likely atelectasis within the left lower lobe.  The cardiac and
 mediastinal silhouette is unremarkable.  Visualized osseous structures are
 unremarkable.
 
 IMPRESSION:  No acute cardiopulmonary process.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old man with s/- AVR/MVR/MAZE  // eval infiltrate/pulm edema      eval infiltrate/pulm edema

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Heart size is enlarged.  Mediastinum is stable.  Replaced aortic and mitral valve are in expected positions.  No pneumothorax is seen.  No increase in pleural effusion demonstrated.

IMPRESSION:

There is interval improvement in bibasal aeration.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST RADIOGRAPH PERFORMED ON [DATE].

INDICATION: Cough and rhinitis, question pneumonia.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: Comparison is made with a prior study from [DATE] as well as a CT chest from [DATE].

FINDINGS:

PA and lateral views of the chest are obtained. The lungs are clear and well expanded without focal consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax. Cardiomediastinal silhouette is normal. Bony structures are intact. No free air below the right hemidiaphragm is seen.

IMPRESSION:

No acute intrathoracic process.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: Chest pain.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Single AP portable view of the chest was obtained. Subtle nodule opacities in the right lower lung and possibly left upper lung. There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax. Cardiomediastinal silhouette is moderately enlarged. There are no bony abnormalities. There is no free air below the right hemidiaphragm. Sternal EKG leads are seen.

IMPRESSION:

Subtle nodular opacities in the right lower lung and possibly left upper lung could be early pneumonia. Follow-up to resolution is recommended. Moderate cardiomegaly.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest: Frontal and lateral views

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]m hyperglycemia today in the context of not taking insulin x3 days. please eval for any cardiopulm change  // [AGE/GENDER]m hyperglycemia today in the context of not taking insulin x3 days. please eval for any cardiopulm change

TECHNIQUE: Chest: Frontal and Lateral

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Subtle lower lobe opacities are seen which may be due to atelectasis, aspiration, or infection. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen. The cardiac silhouette is top-normal. Mediastinal contours are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

Subtle lower lobe opacities may be due to atelectasis, aspiration, subtle infection not excluded.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest PA and lateral

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]-year-old female patient with L1 vertebral body compression fracture and anterior chest pain, not cardiac in nature. Questionable malignancy, bone destruction. Pathological versus degenerative.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Patient's clinical condition required examination in sitting position using AP frontal and left lateral views. There is mild cardiac enlargement. The configuration suggests a prominence of the left ventricular contour to the left and posteriorly, a finding which in conjunction with the moderately widened and generally elongated thoracic aorta suggests the possibility of systemic hypertension. The pulmonary vasculature, however, is not congested and there is no evidence of acute or chronic pulmonary parenchymal infiltrates. The lateral and posterior pleural sinuses are free and no pneumothorax is identified in the apical area. Skeleton skeletal structures are grossly within normal limits. Because of patient's prominent ___, the area of the lower thoracic and upper lumbar spine cannot be identified with any detail. For the conclusive diagnosis of a fracture in this area, dedicated lumbar spine examination is recommended. Our records include multiple previous chest examinations, most of which were taken in [DATE]. On these frontal chest examinations, there is no evidence of any compression fracture of any vertebral body.

IMPRESSION:

For the conclusive diagnosis of a fracture in this area, dedicated lumbar spine examination is recommended.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: chest radiographs

INDICATION: Evaluation of the patient with congestive heart failure.

TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral upright chest radiographs

COMPARISON: in comparison to [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

Cardiomegaly is severe. Mediastinal contours are unchanged, dilated. There is upper zone redistribution, mild, but no overt pulmonary edema. Left mid lung opacity is concerning for infectious process. There is also evidence of bilateral pleural effusions.

IMPRESSION:

Severe cardiomegaly, unchanged dilated mediastinal contours, mild upper zone redistribution without overt pulmonary edema, left mid lung opacity concerning for infectious process, and bilateral pleural effusions.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: ___ is a medically complex [AGE/GENDER]year-old woman with a history of breast cancer, s/p partial mastectomy and chemotherapy (last cycle [DATE]), severe MR, and recent onset HFpEF presumably due to MR. ___ has also had recent PNA and C. dif infections. She was readmitted to ___ in decompensated HF and is being transferred for HF optimization and consideration of surgical candidacy for MVR. // r/o acute process, assess pulm edema, r/o infection

TECHNIQUE: Chest PA and lateral

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

Compared to the prior study there is no significant interval change. There continues to be increase in interstitial markings, pulmonary vascular redistribution, and left effusion. The right-sided catheter tip is in the mid SVC. There is no pneumothorax. Degenerative changes are again seen in the right humeral head.

IMPRESSION:

No chain

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: chest X-ray

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman with hypoxic respiratory failure and septic shock // replaced OGT- positioning

TECHNIQUE: AP portable chest radiograph

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY] from earlier in the day

FINDINGS:

The tip of the endotracheal tube projects 2.3 cm from the carina. The gastric tube extends below the level the diaphragms but beyond the field of view of this radiograph. Large layering pleural effusions are unchanged in addition to bibasilar consolidations. No pneumothorax is identified. The size and appearance of the cardiomediastinal silhouette is unchanged.

IMPRESSION:

No significant interval change since the prior examination.

FINAL REPORT
WET READ: [AGE/GENDER] [AGE/GENDER] [AGE/GENDER] 6:03 PM
  enlarged cardiac silhouette- may be cardiomegaly or possible pericardial
  effusion, can distinguish by echo.
  mild height loss of mid thoracic vertebral body, age indeterminate
______________________________________________________________________________
EXAMINATION: CHEST, TWO VIEWS: [AGE/GENDER]

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]-year-old female with history of AFib and fevers with cough.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Frontal and lateral views of the chest. The lungs are clear of confluent consolidation, effusion, or pulmonary vascular congestion. There is moderate-to-severe enlargement of the cardiac silhouette. Mild compression deformity is seen in the mid thoracic spine which is age indeterminate.

IMPRESSION:

1. Enlargement of the cardiac silhouette which may be due to cardiomegaly, although pericardial effusion is also possible. Consider echocardiogram. 2. Mild height loss of a mid thoracic vertebral body, age indeterminate and clinical correlation is suggested.

FINAL REPORT
WET READ: WET READ: [AGE/GENDER] [DATE] [DATE] 7:07 PM Increased opacity in the hila of the right upper lobe could represent progression of the patients chronic sarcoidosis or an acute overlying pneumonia. ___ ___ d/w ___ ___ MD via telephone at 5:30 pm on [DATE].
______________________________________________________________________________
EXAMINATION: PA AND LATERAL CHEST ON [DATE]

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]-year-old woman with sarcoid and frequent recent pneumonia, with cough and sputum production.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: PA and lateral chest compared to [PRIOR_STUDY] through [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

There is greater consolidation in the right upper lobe joining a region of probable sarcoid scarring, as well as in the right middle lobe, better appreciated on the lateral view, compared to [PRIOR_STUDY]. There has been no change in the extent of central adenopathy, and there is no pleural effusion. Heart size is normal.

IMPRESSION:

Findings are certainly consistent with acute pneumonia, but given the geographic association of the upper lobe infection with a region of chronic abnormality, this area needs to be followed closely to make sure there is no indolent infection, in a region of sarcoid involvement, or any structural explanation such as bronchial stricture.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PA AND LAT)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman with fever, aspiration  // ? aspiration      ? aspiration

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: Comparison to [PRIOR_STUDY].

FINDINGS:

In the interval, the patient has developed bilateral pleural effusions, left more than right. In addition, there is a retrocardiac atelectasis as well as a parenchymal opacity at the right lung base, combined to air bronchograms and thickening of the peribronchial connective tissue in the appropriate clinical setting this change could reflect pneumonia. No pulmonary edema. Unchanged borderline size of the heart.

IMPRESSION:

Not specified.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]F ESRD on HD, COPD, now with hypotension and productive cough now s/p 3L IVF  // Please assess for pneumonia vs pulmonary edema

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

Minimal scarring at both the left and the right lung bases. Non thickening of the minor fissure. No overt pulmonary edema. The cystic findings seen on an accompanying CT examination, reflecting both LIP and centrilobular emphysema are not visualized on this image. Normal size of the heart.

IMPRESSION:

As compared to the previous radiograph, no relevant change is seen.

FINAL REPORT
WET READ: WET READ: [AGE/GENDER] [DATE] 3:59 AM
______________________________________________________________________________
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: History: [AGE/GENDER]M with sob pls ev al pna  // History: [AGE/GENDER]M with sob pls ev al pna

TECHNIQUE: Chest PA and lateral

COMPARISON: Chest radiograph dated [DATE] and CTA chest dated [DATE].

FINDINGS:

Mild right basilar atelectasis. Cardiomediastinal and hilar contours are unchanged. There is no pneumothorax, pleural effusion, or consolidation. The aorta is tortuous.

IMPRESSION:

No pneumonia.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: Trauma, fall

TECHNIQUE: AP view of the chest

COMPARISON: None available

FINDINGS:

There is no focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. The cardiomediastinal contours are normal. No fracture is identified

IMPRESSION:

No acute intrathoracic process. No fracture however a solitary chest radiograph is not designed to evaluate for fracture. Dedicated views based on physical exam findings can be done

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST RADIOGRAPH

INDICATION: Weakness and palpitations

TECHNIQUE: Chest, PA and lateral

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

The heart is again moderately enlarged.  The mediastinal and hilar contours appear stable.  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  The lungs appear clear.

IMPRESSION:

No evidence of acute cardiopulmonary disease

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: chest

INDICATION: History: [AGE/GENDER] with cp  // ptx?

TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral chest

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Lungs are clear. No pneumothorax. Heart size and mediastinal contour are normal. No suspicious bone findings with mild multilevel disc degeneration.

IMPRESSION:

No acute cardiopulmonary abnormalities identified. Mild multilevel disc degeneration noted.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] with fever, right upper quadrant pain, evaluate for pneumonia.

TECHNIQUE: Frontal lateral view of the chest.

COMPARISON: None Available.

FINDINGS:

SINCE [DATE], PREVIOUS MILD PULMONARY EDEMA HAS RESOLVED.  No new focal consolidation.  There is blunting of the left and right costophrenic angles representing small bilateral pleural effusions. ALLOWING FOR DIFFERENCES IN PROJECTION, MILD TO MODERATE CARDIOMEGALY IS UNCHANGED. .  There is no evidence of pneumothorax.  Left-sided 2 lead pacer read demonstrated with leads terminating in the right atrium and right ventricle.  Aortic valve prosthesis is unchanged.

IMPRESSION:

1.  PREVIOUS MILD PULMONARY EDEMA RESOLVED.
2. Small bilateral pleural effusions

FINAL REPORT
WET READ: [AGE/GENDER] [AGE/GENDER] [AGE/GENDER] 2:32 PM
______________________________________________________________________________
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]M with chest tightness  // eval for ptx

TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral views of the chest.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

The lungs are clear.  There is no effusion or pneumothorax.  The cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits.  There is a posterior right eighth rib fracture which is likely old.

IMPRESSION:

No acute cardiopulmonary process.  Probably old posterior right eighth rib fracture.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old man with CHF having desaturations to high 80s  // ? worsening pulm edema vs pneumonia?

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: [DATE], 06:13

FINDINGS:

As compared to the previous radiograph, centralized pulmonary edema has increased in severity. Unchanged moderate cardiomegaly. Status post CABG. No larger pleural effusions. No pneumonia.

IMPRESSION:

Centralized pulmonary edema has increased in severity.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] with AML. Evaluation for allogenic transplant workup.

TECHNIQUE: PA and lateral views of the chest are obtained.

COMPARISON: Comparison is made to chest radiograph from [DATE].

FINDINGS:

The lungs are well expanded and clear. The cardiomediastinal silhouette is unremarkable. There is no evidence of pneumonia, pneumothorax or pleural effusion. The visualized osseous structures are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

Normal chest radiograph

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: Weakness. Evaluate for pneumonia.

TECHNIQUE: Frontal AP and lateral chest radiographs were obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest radiograph from [PRIOR_STUDY].

FINDINGS:

The lungs are well expanded. Nodular opacities projecting over the right hila is unchanged since at least [DATE] and may represent prominent vessel on end versus hilar lymphadenoapthy. No focal parenchymal opacities are seen bilaterally. Mild vascular cephalization is present. The heart is moderately enlarged, and appears slightly worsened compared with [PRIOR_STUDY]. An AICD generator is noted overlying the left upper hemithorax, with three leads ending in expected positions. Sternotomy wires are intact. Ring markers from prior CABG surgery are also stable. There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

IMPRESSION:

Mild vascular congestion in the setting of moderate cardiomegaly. The cardiomegaly appears slightly worsened from [PRIOR_STUDY].

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] with LIJ placement // evaluate LIJ placement

TECHNIQUE: Single portable view of the chest.

COMPARISON: Chest x-ray from earlier the same day at 16:30.

FINDINGS:

There has been interval placement of a left-sided central venous catheter with tip in the upper SVC. There is no pneumothorax. Otherwise, there has been no change.

IMPRESSION:

Otherwise, there has been no change.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest x-ray PA and lateral

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman with history of asthma, CHF, increased dyspnea // ? Worsening CHF, pneumonia

TECHNIQUE: Chest PA and lateral

COMPARISON: Comparison is made to chest x-rays dating from [DATE] through [DATE].

FINDINGS:

The cardiac silhouette is mildly enlarged and stable from previous studies. The aorta is heavily calcified otherwise the mediastinal silhouette is unremarkable. The hila are normal. The lungs are hyperinflated with flattening of the diaphragms. There is a small left pleural effusion but no evidence of focal consolidations or pulmonary edema.

IMPRESSION:

Small left pleural effusion otherwise no acute cardiopulmonary abnormalities.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: History of seizure. Please evaluate for infection.

TECHNIQUE: AP and lateral radiographs of the chest.

COMPARISON: Chest radiograph from [DATE].

FINDINGS:

Mild cardiomegaly has been stable compared to the prior exam from [DATE]. The evaluation of the lung parenchyma is limited due to low lung volumes and patient rotation, particularly of the left lower lobe. There is no large pleural effusion or pneumothorax. The visualized osseous structures are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

Limited assessment of the left lower lobe due to patient rotation, however no definite abnormalities are identified. Recommend repeat true upright and lateral radiographs for further evaluation.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest: Frontal and lateral views

INDICATION: History: [AGE/GENDER] with chest pain and fever  // ?pneumonia

TECHNIQUE: Chest: Frontal and Lateral

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

There relatively low lung volumes.The lungs are clear without focal consolidation.  No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen. The cardiac and mediastinal silhouettes are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

No focal consolidation to suggest pneumonia.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: PORTABLE AP CHEST X-RAY

INDICATION: Patient with intubation.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY] [PRIOR_STUDY].

FINDINGS:

ET tube is in adequate position, ending 6.4 cm above the carina.  Bibasilar opacities are nonspecific, it could represent aspiration pneumonia, atelectasis with superimposed pleural effusion.  There is mild volume overload and moderate cardiomegaly.  There is no pneumothorax.

IMPRESSION:

1.  ET tube is in adequate position. 2.  Bilateral basal opacities are nonspecific and could be due to aspiration, atelectasis or pneumonia with probable superimposed pleural effusion.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old man with s/p AVR // eval PICC placement

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: As compared to previous radiograph of [DATE]

FINDINGS:

a right PICC has been placed, terminating at the expected location of the cavoatrial junction.

IMPRESSION:

Otherwise relatively similar appearance of the chest except for improved aeration at the lung bases.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Not specified.

INDICATION: Cough, suspected aspiration event.

TECHNIQUE: Portable AP radiograph of the chest was reviewed

COMPARISON: in comparison to [PRIOR_STUDY] obtained at [DATE]

FINDINGS:

The current study continues to redemonstrate low lung volumes. There is no evidence of pneumothorax. There is minimal amount of pleural effusion demonstrated. The lungs are essentially clear with no definitive evidence of new consolidation to suggest aspiration process.

IMPRESSION:

no definitive evidence of new consolidation to suggest aspiration process

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]-year-old female with cough and fever. Effusion seen on recent chest x-ray. Evaluation for interval change.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: Comparison is made to radiograph of the chest from [DATE].

FINDINGS:

PA and lateral views of the chest are obtained. There is interval improvement in the right pleural effusion with some underlying atelectasis at the right lung base. The previously seen left pleural effusion is unchanged. The cardiomediastinal silhouette is unremarkable. There is no evidence of pulmonary edema or pneumothorax.

IMPRESSION:

Improving right pleural effusion with underlying atelectasis. Left pleural effusion is unchanged.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST RADIOGRAPH

INDICATION: Dyspnea.

TECHNIQUE: Chest, AP supine.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

The aorta is calcified. Heart appears mildly enlarged. The chest is hyperinflated. Postsurgical changes are noted along the lower right hemithorax. Bronchovascular irregularity suggests emphysema. Patchy retrocardiac opacity suggests opacification in the left lower lobe with mild associated volume loss. Mild loss in height of T12 is noted.

IMPRESSION:

Findings suggesting emphysema. Patchy opacity at the left lung base, probably atelectasis but not specific.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: History: [AGE/GENDER] with cough, presenting status post syncope

TECHNIQUE: Portable upright AP view of the chest

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Lung volumes are low. This accentuates the size of the cardiac silhouette which is borderline enlarged. The mediastinal and hilar contours are unremarkable. There is crowding of the bronchovascular structures, but no overt pulmonary edema is demonstrated. Minimal streaky opacities are seen in the lung bases likely reflective of atelectasis. No large pleural effusion, focal consolidation or pneumothorax is present. Mild to moderate multilevel degenerative changes are noted in the thoracic spine.

IMPRESSION:

Low lung volumes with mild bibasilar atelectasis.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman with tachypnea, chest pain  // r/o edema

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

Rotated positioning. Compared with [PRIOR_STUDY], there has been some interval clearing at both bases, with decreased retrocardiac opacity and renewed visualization of the left hemidiaphragm. Air bronchograms at the right base have also resolved. Small residual left-greater-than-right patchy opacity remains visible. Previously seen vascular blurring, consistent with mild CHF, has resolved. Enlarged cardiomediastinal silhouette, with sternotomy wires and prosthetic valve again noted, unchanged.

IMPRESSION:

Interval improvement of opacities at both bases and interval improvement of CHF findings.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman with stroke  // ?PNA      ?PNA

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: There no prior chest radiographs.

FINDINGS:

Lungs are fully expanded and clear.  Cardiomediastinal and hilar silhouettes and pleural surfaces are normal.

IMPRESSION:

Not specified.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman with SOB, desat  // eval for acute process

TECHNIQUE: CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

Tracheostomy is in place. Cardiomediastinal silhouette is stable. Left pleural effusion is moderate to large, slightly increased. Left perihilar area appears to be increased as well as there is worsening of vascular congestion of the right lung

IMPRESSION:

findings concerning for progression of interstitial pulmonary edema

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest PA and lateral

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER]-year-old male patient with persistent fevers, status post complicated hospital course for mesenteric ischemia, and [DATE] fungemia, evaluate for pneumonia.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS:

PA and lateral chest views were obtained with patient in upright position. Comparison is made with the next preceding portable AP single view chest examination obtained four hours earlier during the same day. The patient was now brought in upright position and a PA and lateral chest image could be obtained. Again, high positioned diaphragm probably related to abdominal process, obscured partially the heart shadow. Significant cardiac enlargement is unlikely. Unremarkable appearance of thoracic aorta without evidence of local contour abnormalities. Unchanged position of previously described PICC line terminating in lower third of SVC. The lateral and posterior pleural sinuses are free from any significant fluid accumulation. Again, there are bilateral thin plate atelectasis related to the high positioned diaphragms, but acute infiltrates of pneumonic appearance cannot be identified. No pneumothorax is seen in the apical area.

IMPRESSION:

No acute pneumonia in patient with history of fungemia in the presence of mesenteric ischemia.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: CHEST RADIOGRAPH

INDICATION: Acute shortness of breath, evaluation for pulmonary edema.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: [PRIOR_STUDY].

FINDINGS:

As compared to the previous radiograph, the aortic balloon pump has been removed. Mild pulmonary edema persists but has not increased as compared to the previous examination. Mild retrocardiac atelectasis, small cardiac silhouette. No pleural effusions.

IMPRESSION:

Mild pulmonary edema persists but has not increased as compared to the previous examination.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: chest

INDICATION: Evaluation of the patient after aortic valve replacement and CABG

TECHNIQUE: AP radiograph of the chest

COMPARISON: compared to [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

interval progression of interstitial pulmonary edema. Minimal, but overall, the severity of the edema is at least moderate, associated with bilateral pleural effusions and bibasal atelectasis. No appreciable pneumothorax is seen. The Dobbhoff tube tip is in the stomach

IMPRESSION:

Progression of interstitial pulmonary edema with at least moderate severity, bilateral pleural effusions, bibasal atelectasis, and appropriately placed Dobbhoff tube

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: [AGE/GENDER] year old woman s/p Chest tube removal with concern for R PTX and subjective SOB // please eval for PTX

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: Radiographs from [PRIOR_STUDY]

FINDINGS:

Support lines and tubes are unchanged in position. Cardiac silhouette is upper limits of normal. There is a persistent moderate-sized left pleural effusion.

IMPRESSION:

There are no pneumothoraces.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest radiograph

INDICATION: Evaluation of NG tube placement in a patient status post open sigmoid colectomy for diverticulitis.

TECHNIQUE: Not specified.

COMPARISON: Chest radiograph, [DATE].

FINDINGS:

Portable AP semi-upright view of the chest was reviewed and compared to the prior study. The nasogastric tube is in proper position with its most proximal side port located in the stomach. There are two additional lines that are known to be external. Left lower lung atelectasis is minimal; otherwise, the lungs are clear. Normal heart, pleural, and mediastinal surfaces.

IMPRESSION:

Nasogastric tube is in proper position with the most proximal side port located in the stomach.

FINAL REPORT
EXAMINATION: Chest X-ray

INDICATION: Shortness of breath. Evaluate for infiltrate.

TECHNIQUE: Portable semi-upright AP radiograph of the chest.

COMPARISON: Chest radiographs, [DATE], [DATE], and [PRIOR_STUDY].

FINDINGS:

The lungs are normally expanded and clear. There is no focal airspace opacity, large pleural effusion or pneumothorax. The heart is not enlarged. The mediastinal and hilar contours are normal. The aortic knob is calcified. A right humeral prosthesis is partially imaged.

IMPRESSION:

No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality.

FINAL REPORT

COMPARISON: Comparison is made with the next preceding similar study of [DATE].

On the previous examination, a right-sided PICC line was identified and seen to terminate overlying the mid portion of the right atrium. The present examination obtained five days later demonstrates the presence of a right-sided PICC line presumably identical with the previous one.
